sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Brandon, a 38-year-old male, presents with neck pain after a recent car crash in February of this year.
He reports a history of multiple car crashes, including the current one, and has been experiencing sharp, incapacitating pain, rated as a 12 out of 10.
He reports stiffness, headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness in his left arm and right leg, and muscle spasms.
He also reports bruising and swelling.
He has been experiencing fatigue since the accident.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of previous car crashes and has undergone surgery after the third crash.
He has also been to a pain clinic and has been prescribed fentanyl, which he has not taken in several weeks.
Review of systems: The patient reports headaches, dizziness, visual disturbances, numbness, and muscle spasms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's neck pain is exacerbated by palpation of the bony process and muscle.
He has a moderate range of motion in his neck, but bending forward and backwards causes significant pain.
No bruising, swelling, or laceration is noted.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a neck sprain, which is a condition that can be caused by multiple factors, including car crashes.
Problem list: Neck sprain; Active.
Plan:
I prescribed Robaxin 1500mg, taken 6-8 hours, 6-8 hours, to help relax the muscles and reduce pain.
I also recommended the patient apply ice to the area when experiencing bruising and swelling, and to perform exercises to improve range of motion.
The patient will undergo an MRI to further evaluate the extent of the injury.
I will refer the patient to physical therapy and pain management to explore local injections and alternative pain relief options.
The patient will be given time off work to recover.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen after the MRI results are available, and further treatment will be planned accordingly.
